results.name: max_run_aversion
results.description: Proportion of trials where the sequence with the shorter normalized maximum run is chosen, conditioned on similar alternation rates (|p_alts_a - p_alts_b| <= 0.1).

results.name: alt_motifs_aversion
results.description: Proportion of trials where the sequence with a lower density of alternating motifs (alt_motifs / n) is chosen, conditioned on similar alternation rates and imbalance.
results.error: ValueError: operands could not be broadcast together with shapes (3840,) (120,) (120,)
results.code:
# name: alt_motifs_aversion
# description: Proportion of trials where the sequence with a lower density of alternating motifs (alt_motifs / n) is chosen, conditioned on similar alternation rates and imbalance.
import numpy as np


def test_statistic(df):
    dens_a = df["alt_motifs_a"] / df["n_a"]
    dens_b = df["alt_motifs_b"] / df["n_b"]
    mask = (
        (np.abs(df["p_alts_a"] - df["p_alts_b"]) <= 0.1)
        & (np.abs(df["imbalance_a"] - df["imbalance_b"]) <= 0.1)
        & (dens_a != dens_b)
    )
    sub = df[mask]
    if len(sub) == 0:
        return np.nan

    chose_less_alt = np.where(dens_a < dens_b, sub["chose_left"], sub["chose_right"])
    return float(np.mean(chose_less_alt))

results.name: prototypical_length_preference
results.description: Proportion of trials where the longer sequence is chosen, conditioned on both sequences being prototypical (imbalance < 0.25 and 0.4 <= p_alts <= 0.6) and having different lengths.
results.code:
# name: prototypical_length_preference
# description: Proportion of trials where the longer sequence is chosen, conditioned on both sequences being prototypical (imbalance < 0.25 and 0.4 <= p_alts <= 0.6) and having different lengths.
import numpy as np


def test_statistic(df):
    mask = (
        (df["imbalance_a"] < 0.25)
        & (df["imbalance_b"] < 0.25)
        & (df["p_alts_a"] >= 0.4)
        & (df["p_alts_a"] <= 0.6)
        & (df["p_alts_b"] >= 0.4)
        & (df["p_alts_b"] <= 0.6)
        & (df["n_a"] != df["n_b"])
    )
    sub = df[mask]
    if len(sub) == 0:
        return np.nan

    chose_longer = np.where(
        sub["n_a"] > sub["n_b"], sub["chose_left"], sub["chose_right"]
    )
    return float(np.mean(chose_longer))

results.name: rep_motifs_aversion
results.description: Proportion of trials where the sequence with a lower density of repeated motifs (rep_motifs / n) is chosen, conditioned on similar alternation rates and imbalance.

# name: rep_motifs_aversion
# description: Proportion of trials where the sequence with a lower density of repeated motifs (rep_motifs / n) is chosen, conditioned on similar alternation rates and imbalance.
import numpy as np


def test_statistic(df):
    dens_a = df["rep_motifs_a"] / df["n_a"]
    dens_b = df["rep_motifs_b"] / df["n_b"]
    mask = (
        (np.abs(df["p_alts_a"] - df["p_alts_b"]) <= 0.1)
        & (np.abs(df["imbalance_a"] - df["imbalance_b"]) <= 0.1)
        & (dens_a != dens_b)
    )
    sub = df[mask]
    if len(sub) == 0:
        return np.nan

    chose_less_rep = np.where(dens_a < dens_b, sub["chose_left"], sub["chose_right"])
    return float(np.mean(chose_less_rep))

results.name: alternating_length_aversion
results.description: Proportion of trials where the shorter sequence is chosen, conditioned on both sequences being highly alternating (p_alts >= 0.7) and having different lengths.

results.name: imbalanced_length_aversion
results.description: Proportion of trials where the shorter sequence is chosen, conditioned on both sequences being highly imbalanced (imbalance >= 0.5) and having different lengths.

results.name: under_alternation_aversion
results.description: Proportion of trials where a moderately alternating sequence (0.4 <= p_alts <= 0.6) is chosen over a severely under-alternating sequence (p_alts <= 0.2), conditioned on similar imbalance.
results.code:
# name: under_alternation_aversion
# description: Proportion of trials where a moderately alternating sequence (0.4 <= p_alts <= 0.6) is chosen over a severely under-alternating sequence (p_alts <= 0.2), conditioned on similar imbalance.
import numpy as np


def test_statistic(df):
    # A is moderate, B is under-alternating
    mask1 = (
        (df["p_alts_a"] >= 0.4)
        & (df["p_alts_a"] <= 0.6)
        & (df["p_alts_b"] <= 0.2)
        & (np.abs(df["imbalance_a"] - df["imbalance_b"]) <= 0.25)
    )
    # B is moderate, A is under-alternating
    mask2 = (
        (df["p_alts_b"] >= 0.4)
        & (df["p_alts_b"] <= 0.6)
        & (df["p_alts_a"] <= 0.2)
        & (np.abs(df["imbalance_a"] - df["imbalance_b"]) <= 0.25)
    )

    sub1 = df[mask1]
    sub2 = df[mask2]

    total = len(sub1) + len(sub2)
    if total == 0:
        return np.nan

    choices_mod = np.sum(sub1["chose_left"]) + np.sum(sub2["chose_right"])
    return float(choices_mod / total)

results.name: periodicity_aversion
results.description: Proportion of trials where the less periodic sequence is chosen, conditioned on a substantial difference in periodicity (> 0.2) and similar alternation rates (|p_alts_a - p_alts_b| <= 0.1).
